encounter.reason: Antiphospholipid syndrome antibody panel results review, counseling, and specialist referrals
encounter.location: Rocky Mountain Women's Health, Denver, CO

labs.name: lupus anticoagulant screen (dilute Russell viper venom time, dRVVT)
labs.value: weakly positive (with confirmatory mixing study positive)

screening.name: PHQ-2 depression screening total score
screening.interpretation: positive screen (threshold ≥3); patient endorses depressed mood and anhedonia related to recurrent pregnancy losses, significant anxiety

conditions.name: suspected antiphospholipid syndrome
conditions.note: Based on elevated anticardiolipin IgG (48 GPL) and weakly positive lupus anticoagulant. Definitive diagnosis deferred pending confirmatory repeat testing approximately July 2025 per revised Sapporo (Sydney) criteria.

conditions.name: positive depression screening
conditions.note: Positive PHQ-2 screen (score 4). Significant anxiety and depressed mood related to recurrent losses. Currently engaged in therapy through EAP. Recommended continuation of therapy and formal mental health referral if symptoms worsen.

document_references.description: Office visit note documenting APS antibody panel results (elevated anticardiolipin IgG, weakly positive lupus anticoagulant, borderline anti-beta-2 glycoprotein I IgG), clinical reasoning linking three consecutive first-trimester losses with chromosomally normal third conceptus to suspected antiphospholipid syndrome, extensive counseling provided to patient and spouse regarding APS implications and treatment options, positive PHQ-2 screening results, specialist referral plan to maternal-fetal medicine and hematology, and mental health follow-up recommendations.

diagnostic_reports.type: lupus anticoagulant assay (dilute Russell viper venom time with confirmatory mixing study)
observation_names: lupus anticoagulant screen (dilute Russell viper venom time, dRVVT)
